data.title: Assertio regains Nasdaq compliance after bid price recovery
data.description:
• Assertio Holdings (ASRT) said on Monday that it has regained compliance with all applicable Nasdaq listing requirements, including the minimum bid price rule.
  • As of January 12, 2026, Nasdaq has determined that Assertio’s shares met the requirement to maintain a minimum bid price of $1.00 for at least 10 consecutive trading days, closing the matter.
  • The pharmaceutical company said the compliance followed the completion of a reverse stock split in December 2025.
  • ASRT closed +1.41% at $9.32.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: OneSpan acquires Build38
data.description:
• OneSpan (NASDAQ:OSPN) said on Monday it has entered into a definitive agreement to acquire Build38.
  • Following the agreement, the company plans to integrate Build38’s SDK-based RASP technology, enhancing its mobile security portfolio, combined with Build38’s industry expertise and compliance with rigorous global certifications to deliver advanced mobile identity protection and security solutions.
  • The planned transaction is expected to close by March 2026.

data.title: Tech Voices: Nvidia-Lilly AI lab, Apple-Google, Meta's new president
data.description:
Seeking Alpha's roundup of statements, announcements, and remarks that could impact the technology sector. 

  • Nvidia (NVDA) and Eli Lilly (LLY) said they're investing up to $1B over five years to develop an AI-powered lab for drug discovery. 

The lab's infrastructure will be built on Nvidia's (NVDA) BioNeMo platform and Vera Rubin architecture. The companies also plan to utilize robotics and physical AI to speed and scale drug discovery and production. The facility will be located in the San Francisco Bay Area, they said. 

“AI is transforming every industry, and its most profound impact will be in life sciences,” said Nvidia CEO and founder Jensen Huang in a statement.

“Nvidia and Lilly are bringing together the best of our industries to invent a new blueprint for drug discovery—one where scientists can explore vast biological and chemical spaces in silico before a single molecule is made," Huang added.

“Combining our volumes of data and scientific knowledge with Nvidia’s computational power and model-building expertise could reinvent drug discovery as we know it," Lilly Chair and CEO David Ricks said in the statement.

"By bringing together world-class talent in a startup environment, we’re creating the conditions for breakthroughs that neither company could achieve alone," Ricks added.

  • Apple (AAPL) said its future Apple Intelligence features, including an updated version of Siri, will be based on Google's (GOOG) (GOOGL) Gemini under a new multi-year collaboration deal. 

"After careful evaluation, Apple determined that Google's Al technology provides the most capable foundation for Apple Foundation Models and is excited about the innovative new experiences it will unlock for Apple users," Apple said in a blog post.

"Apple Intelligence will continue to run on Apple devices and Private Cloud Compute, while maintaining Apple's industry-leading privacy standards," Apple added.

  • Meta Platforms (META) has hired former Trump advisor Dina Powell McCormick as its president and vice chairman. 

“Dina’s experience at the highest levels of global finance, combined with her deep relationships around the world, makes her uniquely suited to help Meta manage this next phase of growth as the company’s President and Vice Chairman,” said Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg in a statement.

McCormick served as a Meta board member last year but resigned in December. A former partner at Goldman Sachs, she served as deputy national security advisor for the first Trump administration.

data.title: Heritage Global acquires DebtX assets for about $8.5 million
data.description:
• Heritage Global (HGBL) said on Monday that a wholly owned subsidiary has acquired substantially all of the assets of loan-sale advisor The Debt Exchange Inc in an all-cash transaction valued at about $8.5 million.
  • The deal, which was contractually effective on January 1, 2026, expands Heritage Global’s Financial Assets division and its presence in the secondary loan market. DebtX advises on sales of performing and non-performing commercial, real estate and residential loans.
  • Heritage Global said DebtX has more than 20 years of operating history and that the acquisition is expected to be accretive in calendar year 2026, with potential quarterly variability.
  • Following the acquisition, DebtX will continue to be managed by its existing senior leadership team under Heritage DebtX, with co-founder Bruce Hounsell set to assume leadership later this month.
  • HGBL +17.64% after hours to $1.6.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: SciSparc receives Nasdaq notice over stockholders’ equity shortfall
data.description:
• SciSparc (SPRC) on Monday it had received a notification from Nasdaq that it is not in compliance with the minimum stockholders’ equity requirement for continued listing on the Nasdaq Capital Market.
  • The exchange said the clinical-stage pharmaceutical company failed to maintain the required minimum of $2.5 million in stockholders’ equity. SciSparc reported stockholders’ equity of about negative $81,000 as of June 30, 2025, according to a filing dated November 18, 2025.
  • Under Nasdaq rules, SciSparc has until February 26, 2026 to submit a plan to regain compliance. If accepted, Nasdaq may grant an extension of up to 180 days to demonstrate compliance.
  • The notice has no immediate impact on the company’s Nasdaq listing, and its shares will continue to trade under the symbol SPRC during the grace period.
  • SciSparc said it expects to demonstrate compliance as of December 31, 2025 following proceeds from the August 2025 conversion of $4.2 million in debentures and a registered direct offering completed in November 2025.
  • SPRC closed -2.05% at $1.43.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: U.S. regulators say credit risk for big banks remain moderate
data.description:
The U.S. Federal Deposit Insurance Corp., Federal Reserve and Office of the Comptroller of the Currency on Monday said that credit risk associated with large, syndicated loans were still moderate in 2025, as borrowers continued to navigate higher interest expenses and other macroeconomic factors.

The 2025 Shared National Credit Program Report features 6,857 borrowers totaling $6.9T in commitments, up 6% from a year earlier. The review focused on SNC loans originated on or before June 30, 2025.

Meanwhile, the percentage of loans that deserve management's close attention (aka, non-pass loans) fell to 8.6% of total commitments from 9.1% in 2024, the regulators said, driven by growth in new commitments rather than an underlying credit quality boost. 

U.S. lenders hold 45% of all SNC commitments, though they hold only 22% of non-pass loans, Almost half of total SNC commitments are leveraged, and leveraged loans comprise 81% of non-pass loans.

Sitting at the top of the syndicated loan market include JPMorgan Chase (JPM), Citigroup (C), Goldman Sachs (GS) and Morgan Stanley (MS), usually in the form of arrangers and bookrunners.

data.title: ConocoPhillips upgraded on higher dividend growth outlook, Range Resources cut at Wells Fargo
data.description:
ConocoPhillips (COP) closed -2% on Monday despite winning an upgrade from Wells Fargo to Overweight from Equal Weight with a $132 price target, raised from $100, saying shares are positioned for peer-leading multi-year dividend compound annual growth.

Wells Fargo's Sam Margolin said the start-up of the Willow project in 2029 represents a significant free cash flow inflection for Conoco (COP), contributing a ~$4B net change in the first year at $65 Brent prices, but the company also has organic FCF growth, and resultant growth
in dividend capacity in the intervening years driven by the roll off of Qatar LNG spending; the start-up of growth projects, particularly Qatar LNG and Port Arthur LNG; and the FY 2025 Port Arthur dividend payment, contributing to share repurchases the and resulting reduction of gross dividend burden; and a potential compression of Waha gas differentials following the start-up of egress pipeline capacity in 2027.

The analyst sees the net effect resulting in greater than $6/share in cumulative dividend capacity growth between during 2026-29, compared to a 2026 dividend of $3.36/share following an 8% increase at Q3 2025 earnings, a growth trend viewed as comparatively defensive within downside crude price scenarios.

Meanwhile, Margolin downgraded Range Resources (RRC) to Equal Weight from Overweight with a $43 PT, down from $46, citing a relative valuation premium based on free cash flow yield, particularly compared to direct peer Antero Resources (AR).

While Range's (RRC) relative premium on free cash flow is justifiable based on an advantaged balance sheet and cost structure, the premium already achieved "renders upside friction in
an environment of higher risk to gas markets," the analyst said.

data.title: Destination XL Group reports holiday sales of $89.9M
data.description:
• Destination XL Group (DXLG) announced on Monday that its total sales were $89.9M compared to $94.7M for the 9-week holiday sales period ended January 4, 2025.
  • The firm said that its comparable sales for the same 9-week holiday period decreased 5.8%, with comparable sales from stores down 7.2% and the direct business down 2.8%.
  • As announced on December 11, 2025, the company has entered into a definitive agreement to combine with FBB Holdings in a merger of equals to create a scaled, category-defining retailer for inclusive apparel. 
  • The companies delivered combined net sales of approximately $1.2 billion for the last twelve months ending October 2025. Assuming no pro forma adjustments, adjusted EBITDA was approximately $45 million for the last twelve months ending October 2025. Including $25 million in expected run-rate cost synergies, the combined company would have generated approximately $70 million of LTM Adjusted EBITDA.
  • The transaction is expected to close in the first half of fiscal year 2026.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Core CPI expected to see renewed goods strength in December, but softer shelter inflation
data.description:
December's Consumer Price Index comes out on Tuesday, giving markets and economists fresh data on how hot inflation is running. While the December data makes progress in clearing up the fog that resulted from the government's 43-day shutdown, it will still reflect some measurement issues.

The Bureau of Labor Statistic's "zeroing out" of housing costs in October's CPI, a result of the government shutdown, will continue to play out, KPMG US Chief Economist Diane Swonk told Seeking Alpha.

The consensus expects that the December CPI will rise 0.3% M/M, up from +0.2% in November, bringing the expected year-over-year increase to 2.6%, down from 2.7% in the prior month.

Excluding food and energy, core CPI is also seen rising 0.3% M/M vs. +0.2% in November, with the Y/Y comparison showing a 2.7% increase, accelerating from 2.6% in the previous month.

Citi economist Veronica Clark is penciling in a 0.27% M/M increase in core CPI, with strength in goods prices reflecting a rebound from the downward bias inherent in November, "as price collections occurred around holiday season discounts rather than throughout the whole month." Furthermore, seasonality in airfares could increase services prices. 

"But if anything, somewhat softer shelter inflation that would be clean of measurement issues should be an encouraging sign of slowing inflation, and 0.27% as we expect may underwhelm expectations for a larger mechanical rebound," Clark wrote in a note to clients.

Evercore ISI's Stan Shipley estimate a 0.34% M/M increase in CPI and a 0.38% rise in core CPI.  "Our estimate is above consensus as the pricing survey for November was done late when prices are low from holiday sales," he explained.

The consensus of 22V Research investors anticipates core CPI to rise 2.6% or 2.8%, vs. the Bloomberg consensus of 2.7%, 22V's Dennis DeBusschere said in a note. "There is a slight bias towards a higher inflation reading," he said. "In total, 41% of investors we surveyed think core CPI will be below sell-side consensus (bbg 2.7%), 48% think above."

Some 66% of the respondents think core CPI is on a Fed-friendly glide path, that is, without a significant tightening of financial conditions or a recession, which is still near a record high for 22V surveys. "Only 2% expect a recession, the lowest in our survey history."

Meanwhile, the Cleveland Fed Nowcasts for inflation see core CPI up 0.22% M/M in December and up 2.6% Y/Y. Headline CPI is estimated to increase 0.20% M/M and 2.57% Y/Y.

Chris Lau, Investing Group Leader for DIY Value Investing, doesn't expect much of a reaction from the markets after Tuesday's inflation report. "A modest increase in December CPI will not likely change the stock market’s bullish sentiment," he wrote. "The report would need to flag a sharp rise in food and shelter prices to create a panic." 

Still, food and shelter are areas of concern. Consumer discretionary and financials are likely to react the most to the report, he said.

data.title: First Merchants Corporation 7.50% PFD SER Aprice alerts declares $0.4688 dividend
data.description:
• First Merchants Corporation 7.50% PFD SER Aprice alerts (FRMEP) declares $0.4688/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 7.26%
  • Payable Feb. 16; for shareholders of record Jan. 30; ex-div Jan. 30.
  • See FRMEP Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.
